events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate hot work over sewer/drain in-progress. Need sequence check. Need determine decision fail because hot work over drains/sewers is prohibited unless covered/wetted/inerted? NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J. Hot work shall not be performed in areas where flammable vapors, gases, dusts exist; within 35 ft of storage of large quantities of combustibles; over or near sewers/drains where sparks could ignite flammable vapors. Need covers or precautions: cover or plug drains, remove combustibles, fire watch, permit. Since observed hot work over sewer/drain actively in progress, sequence violation: before continuing, cover/seal drain, remove combustibles, inspect area, implement fire watch, obtain hot work permit, ensure no flammable vapors. Component condition installed-defective? The drain exposed/unprotected is installed-defective maybe. Need fill fields. decision fail. risk high or critical? Hot work over sewer/drain can ignite flammable vapors -> critical maybe. But typical classification high. Let's decide high? The prompt says dangerous or non-compliant decision fail. Risk high. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 40. component_conditions ["installed-defective"] because drain not protected. value 0. sound_source empty? Use "" maybe. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0. order: list step IDs in correct sequence? The task asks check order and state what must happen before work can continue. We can provide order of required steps before continuing. Use step ids like ["secure-permit", "inspect-area", "cover-seal-drain", "remove-combustibles", "assign-fire-watch", "perform-hot-work", "post-fire-watch"]. workable maybe empty or activities that can still start? Not asked. Use empty list. findings: ["Hot work being performed directly over an open sewer/drain without covers or inerting", "No evidence of drain sealing to prevent sparks/vapor ignition", "Sequence violation: hot work started before required fire prevention controls"]. actions: ["Stop hot work immediately", "Cover or plug the sewer/drain to prevent sparks entry", "Remove or shield combustibles within 35 ft", "Complete hot work permit and hazard assessment", "Establish trained fire watch with extinguishing equipment", "Test/atmosphere monitor for flammable vapors if suspected", "Reinspect and authorize before resuming"]. rationale brief. references ["NFPA 51B", "OSHA 1926 Subpart J"]. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Drain is not covered, plugged, or inerted to prevent spark/vapor ignition",
    "Required fire-prevention sequence was not completed before hot work started"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop hot work immediately",
    "Cover or plug the sewer/drain to block sparks and vapors",
    "Remove or shield combustibles within the hot-work zone",
    "Complete a hot-work permit and hazard assessment",
    "Assign a trained fire watch with suitable extinguishing equipment",
    "Verify no flammable vapors are present before resuming",
    "Reinspect and authorize restart only after controls are in place"
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J prohibit or strictly control hot work over sewers/drains because sparks can ignite flammable vapors. The observed work skipped the required pre-work controls (permit, drain sealing, combustible removal, fire watch), creating an ignition hazard.",

    "NFPA 51B",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J"
  ]
